summary: The paper makes a genuinely interesting empirical finding (H3 reversed: high-absorption features are more steerable, r=+0.35), but has 3 CRITICAL flaws: (1) H2 pilot claims (70.9% reduction) contradict full-scale results (4.5x INCREASE), driven by incompatible absorption metrics; (2) H5 'directional confirmation' uses a non-existent 5% threshold when the actual criterion was >8%, achieved 2.51%; (3) The entanglement/hub mechanism hypothesis is presented as named contribution without empirical support. The CMI analysis has a qualitative sign reversal across dimensions, probe quality confound is unaddressed, and the 18% steering effect lacks mechanistic validation. The paper has honest negative results (H2 falsified, H4 partially confirmed) which are its strongest aspect.

findings.description: Entanglement hypothesis presented as named contribution without empirical support beyond H3 Spearman r=+0.35. No network analysis performed. 'Hub feature' analogy introduced without evidence. Mechanism claim in abstract ('causally important') overstates the evidence—steering sensitivity measures manipulability, not causal importance per se.
findings.suggestion: Remove named hypothesis or present it as speculative interpretation. Add empirical support (feature activation frequency correlation, decoder direction centrality). Replace 'causally important' with 'highly steerable' or 'computationally central' in abstract.

findings.description: CMI dimension instability: CMI-absorption correlation at d'=10 reverses sign at d'>=20 (d'=10: rho=-0.383, p=0.059 uncorrected; d'=20: rho=+0.048; d'=30: rho=+0.299; d'=50: rho=+0.197). Bonferroni-corrected p=0.236. Sign reversal is qualitative failure, not sensitivity issue. Explanations (post-hoc signal capture at low d', k-NN estimator bias, probe quality confound) are equally plausible.
findings.suggestion: Do not cherry-pick d'=10 as primary dimension. Add ablation and baseline comparisons. Discuss the sign reversal as a limitation. Consider restricting analysis to quality-gated letters (F1>0.85).

findings.description: Probe quality confound in CMI analysis: Absorption rate correlates with probe F1 at rho=-0.67 (p<0.001). CMI-absorption analysis uses rates from L0=82 where mean probe F1=0.817. Low-CMI letters may be inherently harder to probe, causing both low estimated CMI and artificially high absorption rates. Paper never computes partial correlation controlling for probe F1.
findings.suggestion: Compute partial correlation controlling for probe F1. Restrict CMI analysis to 10 quality-gated letters (F1>0.85). Acknowledge this as a limitation in Discussion.

findings.description: OrtSAE ablation (without penalty: 0.230 at L0~920) is compared against OrtSAE with penalty (0.247 at L0~550) to conclude 'the orthogonality penalty does not appear to reduce absorption.' This comparison is at UNMATCHED L0—the very confound the paper criticizes. The ablation conclusion is self-contradictory.
findings.suggestion: Compare OrtSAE variants at matched L0 values. Or compare each variant to vanilla SAE at similar L0. Do not compare OrtSAE-with-penalty to OrtSAE-without-penalty at different L0.

findings.description: The core novelty claim—that L0 confounds architecture comparisons—is important but may be considered a methodological observation rather than research contribution. SAEBench (Karvonen et al., 2025) already compares architectures and notes sparsity differences. The contribution is quantifying the magnitude, not discovering the confound.
findings.suggestion: Reframe the L0 confound as a contribution to experimental methodology, not a standalone research contribution. Emphasize what is genuinely novel about the specific findings.

findings.description: The r~+0.93 sparsity-absorption correlation is based on n=4 data points with TWO points at identical L0=50 (TopK and MultiScale pilot). With two points stacked vertically, the correlation is driven by only 3 unique L0 values. Bootstrap CI does not address this fundamental sample-size limitation.
findings.suggestion: Do not present n=4 correlation as robust finding. Either add more data points or explicitly acknowledge the limitation. Consider using the 4 points as descriptive rather than inferential.

findings.description: MCC ~0.21 across ALL variants including Random control suggests Hungarian matching on overcomplete dictionaries yields chance-level recovery regardless of training. If SAEs are not recovering ground-truth features, what does 'absorption reduction' actually mean? Paper acknowledges MCC failure but does not address the implication.
findings.suggestion: Address the implication: absorption reduction may reflect sparsity-induced suppression rather than genuine hierarchical feature recovery. Discuss what MCC=0.21 means for the validity of all absorption metrics used.

findings.description: Section 3.5 promises one-way ANOVA across all completed variants but this is not reported in Results. With only 3 variants and 5 replicates, ANOVA would have 2,12 df and low power. Focus on pairwise effect sizes is more appropriate but unfulfilled promise undermines credibility.
findings.suggestion: Either report the ANOVA or remove the promise from Section 3.5. If ANOVA was skipped due to low power, explain this transparently.

metric_gaming_risks: H2 absorption metric switches between pilot (first-letter probe, ~0.225 vanilla) and full-scale (Gini, ~0.015 vanilla), making the 70.9% reduction claim untestable
CMI analysis cherry-picks d'=10 as primary dimension where the correlation is negative; d'>=20 shows opposite sign
H5 pass criterion uses non-existent 5% threshold when actual criterion was >8%
novelty_risks: The core H3 finding is a real correlation (r=+0.35) but its mechanism is unvalidated. Entanglement/hub hypothesis is post-hoc and unsupported
The 18% effect size is modest. Without mechanism or Gemma-2B replication, finding could be GPT-2-specific or confounded by feature characteristics correlated with UAS
Tian et al. (2025) 'activation sensitivity' vs our 'steering sensitivity' distinction is vague; both are measuring feature behavior, not causal importance
reproducibility_gaps: CMI-absorption correlation is dimension-dependent (sign reversal at d'>=20). Protocol does not specify which d' is primary
Absorption metric inconsistent between pilot (first-letter probe) and full-scale (Gini). Cross-experiment comparisons are invalid
Feature selection threshold (UAS > 1.0 stated, ~0.57 actual) is undocumented
Null control protocol (alpha=5 only) does not match main experiment (alpha range [1,3,5,10,20])
